source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Tycho.AI

Tycho.AI is a venture-backed pioneer in autonomous intelligence for unmanned systems, delivering breakthrough performance at the edge. Led by serial entrepreneurs with successful exits and roots at MIT and Harvard, we design advanced hardware and software as an integrated whole, enabling AI systems that sense, decide, and act in real-time, without dependence on remote infrastructure. Our modular architectures combine advanced sensing, adaptive AI models, and high-performance edge compute to create resilient, mission-ready autonomy. By uniting hardware optimization with cutting-edge algorithms, Tycho.AI is transforming how AI is built, deployed, and sustained in the world’s most demanding operational environments.

If you’re looking to do the most meaningful technical work of your career, in a culture that values bold ideas, fast execution, and real impact, we want you on our team.

What you’ll do:

You’ll work hands-on with embedded systems, robotics frameworks, and cutting-edge autonomy stacks, and grow fast alongside some of the most experienced minds in AI, autonomy, and defense tech.

 - Develop C++ software for autonomous aerial and ground systems

 - Contribute to real-time software for embedded systems using C and RTOS

 - Integrate advanced autonomy using ROS2 and STM32 Cube platforms

 - Help shape our build pipeline with GitHub Actions, Docker, and CMake

 - Collaborate across autonomy, hardware, and product teams to bring new capabilities to life

 - Learn from (and contribute to) a high-velocity, high-integrity engineering culture

What you bring:

 - BS in Engineering or related technical field

 - 2+ years of C++ experience and CMake proficiency for junior engineers, 5+ years for senior engineers

 - Familiarity with embedded C and RTOS environments

 - Exposure to STM32 Cube and ROS2 (or similar middleware)

 - Experience with Git, GitHub Actions, and Docker

 - Passion for robotics, autonomy, and systems that operate in the real world

Bonus points for:

 - Experience with embedded Linux

 - Prior work on autonomous systems or robotic platforms

We’re not just building technology; we’re building a company that moves fast, tackles tough challenges, and supports one another every step of the way. If you’re ready to help shape the future, apply now.

Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Statement: Tycho AI is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, or gender identity), national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or protected veteran status.

Pay Transparency Nondiscrimination Provision: Tycho AI will not discharge or otherwise discriminate against employees or applicants because they have inquired about, discussed, or disclosed their own pay or the pay of another employee or applicant.

E-Verify and Right to Work: Tycho AI participates in E-Verify to confirm work authorization in the United States. Applicants must be authorized to work in the U.S. and may be subject to a government security clearance process.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TYCHO.AI

Tycho.AI builds autonomy software for unmanned systems operating in GPS- and comms-denied environments. Backed by VCs with unicorn wins and trusted by defense customers, our team is rethinking autonomy from the chip up using modular AI, advanced visual-inertial sensing, and real-time edge compute to unlock next-gen mission capability.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for experienced energetic software engineers to join our growing team building out and delivering on-board and off-board software for state of the art unmanned aerial vehicle products.

As part of the Autonomy Product team you will work closely with high calibre software and hardware engineers to bridge the gap from research to production autonomy systems.

An ideal candidate has experience developing software in a mature collaborative software development environment (ie. with software continuous improvement, simulation and test driven development as well as evaluation pipelines) and is enthusiastic about making a big impact maturing software components into a robust, cohesive, maintainable whole system.

As a software generalist this role spans from going deep implementing specific individual features, creating internal or customer facing interfaces to taking the lead on whole end-to-end capabilities or enabling pipelines.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, develop, and optimize software to enable robotics tasks.

 - Design, develop, and optimize software to interface with internal and external stakeholder software.

 - Integrate new capabilities into real-world platforms, bridging research and production.

 - Write and supervise the development of clean, efficient, and maintainable code in C++ and Python.

 - Contribute to robust software development and test pipelines for large-scale dataset based test evaluation and resimulation.




REQUIREMENTS

 - Bachelor’s or advanced degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field.

 - 3+ years of software development experience in C++ environment.

 - Strong coding skills in C++ or Python (ideally both).

 - Strong software engineering architecture, abstraction and design skills.

 - Hands-on experience applying writing software for robotics and/or computer vision applications.

 - Experience with Robotics middleware (such as ROS2 or protobuf), and Linux based software development.

 - Experience with Git, code review, integration testing, CI/CD.

 - Experience with test driven development, evaluation, integration and burndown testing.

 - Experience implementing software interfaces to other onboard or offboard software.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience working in autonomous driving or similar robot software development environments.

 - Familiarity with robotic software algorithms SLAM, cameras, sensor fusion and/or motion planning.

 - Experience with interfacing to physical devices, sensors, data exchange/network protocols, and realtime systems.

 - Familiarity with bash and python scripting.

 - Experience with Drone software (such as Ardupilot, Betaflight).

 - Familiarity with defense software platforms such as Android Team Awareness Kit (ATAK).




WHY TYCHO.AI

 - Be part of a high calibre rapidly scaling startup defining the future of autonomous intelligence.

 - Make a difference, help create the software development culture and ecosystem you want to develop in.

 - Collaborative, high-trust culture that values innovation, execution, and continuous learning.

 - Competitive salary, meaningful equity, and generous benefits package.




EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY (EEO) STATEMENT:

Tycho AI is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, or gender identity), national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or protected veteran status.




E-VERIFY AND RIGHT TO WORK

Tycho AI participates in E-Verify to confirm work authoriz

source_jobs.title: Autonomy Engineer - Perception
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TYCHO.AI

Tycho.AI is redefining the future of autonomous intelligence. Spun out of MIT and backed by DoD contracts, we are building breakthrough AI and autonomy solutions for unmanned systems operating in GPS-denied and contested environments. We are a fast-growing, dual-use technology company at the forefront of national security and commercial innovation, with a mission to push the boundaries of what autonomous systems can achieve.

Joining Tycho.AI means being part of a team shaping the next decade of autonomy from defense applications to commercial opportunities in areas like logistics, disaster response, and beyond. If you want to work at the cutting edge of AI/ML and robotics with a company that’s poised for major impact and growth, we want to hear from you.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Autonomy Engineer - Perception at Tycho.AI, you will design, build, and validate perception systems for intelligent autonomous vehicles. You will collaborate with a cross-functional team of electrical, mechanical, and software experts to explore new concepts through rapid, iterative prototyping. We are seeking adaptable engineers who collaborate effectively across disciplines, brainstorm innovative ideas, and navigate through emerging embedded technologies. Most importantly, you should have a proven ability to drive a concept through the prototype phase and excel in hardware/software co-development.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, implement, and maintain autonomy software for computer vision and autonomy tasks, including scene reconstruction, object detection, and navigation.

 - Integrate systems into real-world platforms, bridging research and production.

 - Stay current on research and apply state-of-the-art techniques in autonomy and perception.




REQUIREMENTS

 - Master’s or PhD degree in Robotics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field; or equivalent working experience.

 - Proficiency in C/C++ with experience shipping production-grade software.

 - Experience building SLAM, perception, and sensor fusion systems.

 - Strong foundation in both 2D (e.g., object detection, segmentation) and 3D (e.g., multi-view geometry, scene reconstruction) computer vision and perception tasks.

 - Hands-on experience working with real sensor data, particularly in challenging environments, such as night, sea, or underground.

 - Experience with recent developments in ML for computer vision, including ViT and other ML architectures for tasks like segmentation, object detection, classification etc.

 - Comfortable developing and reasoning about multi-threaded real-time systems.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience working with embedded robotics platforms, such as NVIDIA Jetson.

 - Experience optimizing and accelerating ML models for deployment, specifically using techniques like quantization, distillation, or architectural refinement.

 - Familiarity with dense scene reconstruction, like occupancy grids, NeRF, or Gaussian splatting.

 - Familiarity with perception model performance evaluation and evaluation pipelines.




WHY TYCHO.AI

 - Be part of a rapidly scaling startup defining the future of autonomous intelligence.

 - Collaborative, high-trust culture that values innovation, execution, and continuous learning.

 - Make a difference, help create the software development culture and ecosystem you want to develop in.

 - Competitive salary, meaningful equity, and generous benefits package.




EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY (EEO) STATEMENT:

Tycho.AI is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, or gender identity), national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or protected veteran status.




E-VERIFY AND RIGHT TO WORK

Tycho.AI participates in E-Verify to confirm work authorization in the United States. Applicants must be authorized to work in the U.S. and may be su

source_jobs.title: Autonomy Engineer - Motion Planning & Controls
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TYCHO.AI

Tycho.AI is redefining the future of autonomous intelligence. Spun out of MIT and backed by DoD contracts, we are building breakthrough AI and autonomy solutions for unmanned systems operating in GPS-denied and contested environments. We are a fast-growing, dual-use technology company at the forefront of national security and commercial innovation, with a mission to push the boundaries of what autonomous systems can achieve.

Joining Tycho.AI means being part of a team shaping the next decade of autonomy from defense applications to commercial opportunities in areas like logistics, disaster response, and beyond. If you want to work at the cutting edge of AI/ML and robotics with a company that’s poised for major impact and growth, we want to hear from you.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Autonomy Engineer - Motion Planning & Controls at Tycho.AI, you will design, build, and validate motion planning and control systems for intelligent autonomous vehicles. You will collaborate with a cross-functional team of electrical, mechanical, and software experts to explore new concepts through rapid, iterative prototyping. We are seeking adaptable engineers who collaborate effectively across disciplines, brainstorm innovative ideas, and navigate through emerging embedded technologies. Most importantly, you should have a proven ability to drive a concept through the prototype phase and excel in hardware/software co-development.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, implement, and maintain autonomy software for motion planning, trajectory optimization, and flight control.

 - Integrate systems into real-world platforms, bridging research and production.

 - Stay current on research and apply state-of-the-art techniques.




REQUIREMENTS

 - Master’s or PhD degree in Engineering, Robotics, Computer Science, or a related field; or equivalent working experience.

 - Proficiency in C/C++ with experience shipping production-grade software.

 - Strong foundation in modern control system design, including MPC.

 - Experience developing and implementing motion planning and trajectory optimization systems.

 - Working understanding rotorcraft and fixed-wing aircraft flight dynamics.

 - Hands-on experience implementing motion planning and control software on real robotics vehicles.

 - Comfortable developing and reasoning about multi-threaded real-time systems.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Familiarity with learned motion planning and/or data-driven trajectory improvement techniques.

 - Familiarity with high-speed vehicle planning or multi-agent trajectory optimization.

 - Experience working with embedded robotics platforms, such as NVIDIA Jetson.

 - Experience writing microcontroller code, e.g., on STM32.

 - Experience building vehicle dynamics simulations based on flight test data.

 - Familiarity with open-source flight control systems, such as Ardupilot and PX4.

 - Background in convex and nonlinear optimization.




WHY TYCHO.AI

 - Be part of a rapidly scaling startup defining the future of autonomous intelligence.

 - Collaborative, high-trust culture that values innovation, execution, and continuous learning.

 - Competitive salary, meaningful equity, and generous benefits package.




EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY (EEO) STATEMENT:

Tycho.AI is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, or gender identity), national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or protected veteran status.




E-VERIFY AND RIGHT TO WORK

Tycho.AI participates in E-Verify to confirm work authorization in the United States. Applicants must be authorized to work in the U.S. and may be subject to a government security clearance process.

source_jobs.title: Autonomy Engineer - State Estimation
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT TYCHO.AI

Tycho.AI is redefining the future of autonomous intelligence. Spun out of MIT and backed by DoD contracts, we are building breakthrough AI and autonomy solutions for unmanned systems operating in GPS-denied and contested environments. We are a fast-growing, dual-use technology company at the forefront of national security and commercial innovation, with a mission to push the boundaries of what autonomous systems can achieve.

Joining Tycho.AI means being part of a team shaping the next decade of autonomy from defense applications to commercial opportunities in areas like logistics, disaster response, and beyond. If you want to work at the cutting edge of AI/ML and robotics with a company that’s poised for major impact and growth, we want to hear from you.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Autonomy Engineer - State Estimation at Tycho.AI, you will design, build, and validate state estimation systems for intelligent autonomous vehicles. You will collaborate with a cross-functional team of electrical, mechanical, and software experts to explore new concepts through rapid, iterative prototyping. We are seeking adaptable engineers who collaborate effectively across disciplines, brainstorm innovative ideas, and navigate through emerging embedded technologies. Most importantly, you should have a proven ability to drive a concept through the prototype phase and excel in hardware/software co-development.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, implement, and maintain autonomy software for motion planning, trajectory optimization, and flight control.

 - Integrate systems into real-world platforms, bridging research and production.

 - Stay current on research and apply state-of-the-art techniques.




REQUIREMENTS

 - Master’s or PhD degree in Engineering, Robotics, Computer Science, or a related field; or equivalent working experience.

 - Proficiency in C/C++ with experience shipping production-grade software.

 - Strong foundation in state estimation algorithms, including Kalman filters, complementary filters, and optimization-based smoothing (e.g., using factor graphs).

 - Experience with GPS-denied and visual navigation techniques, such as visual-inertial odometry (VIO).

 - Solid background in linear algebra, probability theory, and optimization.

 - Working understanding of rotorcraft and fixed-wing aircraft flight dynamics.

 - Hands-on experience implementing state estimation software on real robotics vehicles.

 - Comfortable developing and reasoning about multi-threaded real-time systems.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience writing microcontroller code and working with embedded robotics platforms, such as NVIDIA Jetson.

 - Experience building vehicle dynamics simulations based on flight test data.

 - Experience with multi-modal sensor fusion and calibration.

 - Experience with target detection and tracking, preferably using multi-modal sensor data.

 - Familiarity with open-source flight control systems, such as Ardupilot and PX4.




WHY TYCHO.AI

 - Be part of a rapidly scaling startup defining the future of autonomous intelligence.

 - Collaborative, high-trust culture that values innovation, execution, and continuous learning.

 - Competitive salary, meaningful equity, and generous benefits package.




EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY (EEO) STATEMENT:

Tycho.AI is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, or gender identity), national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or protected veteran status.




E-VERIFY AND RIGHT TO WORK

Tycho.AI participates in E-Verify to confirm work authorization in the United States. Applicants must be authorized to work in the U.S. and may be subject to a government security clearance process.

notes: Tycho.AI is a venture-backed pioneer in autonomous intelligence for unmanned systems.
